original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Urinary incontinence and pelvic organ prolapse in women: management
original_registry_entry.version: NICE NG123; minor links and language updates through March 2025
original_registry_entry.population: Women aged 18 and over with urinary incontinence or overactive-bladder symptoms, with UTI, voiding, referral, and treatment qualifiers.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Initial assessment, urine testing, bladder diaries, conservative management, and specialist referral.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Partial for a sex-unspecified frequency or urgency workflow. Exact only for selected female urinary-incontinence or overactive-bladder assessment and clinician-documented conservative-management fields; medicines, procedures, fixed follow-up, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_sections.heading: Assessing urinary incontinence — history and physical examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports urgency-incontinence or overactive-bladder symptom context, relevant contributing factors, and clinician-performed pelvic-floor assessment in adult women.

exact_sections.heading: Urine testing, residual urine, and bladder diaries
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports urine dipstick, qualified culture, post-void residual, and bladder-diary documentation in adult women.

exact_sections.heading: Indications for referral to a specialist service
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified bladder, urethral, voiding, neurological, haematuria, or recurrent unexplained UTI contexts.

exact_sections.heading: Lifestyle and behavioural interventions
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting caffeine or fluid discussion and bladder training only when discussed or arranged by the clinician for qualified adult women.

title: Urinary incontinence and pelvic organ prolapse in women: management
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
population: Women aged 18 and over with urinary incontinence or overactive-bladder symptoms, with UTI, voiding, referral, and treatment qualifiers.
intended_setting: Initial assessment, urine testing, bladder diaries, conservative management, and specialist referral.
